name: Soused Oysters - Ostiones En Escabeche
recipeIngredient: 3 Fresh marjoram sprigs or 1/4 tsp Dried marjoram
1 1/2 tbsp Dried oregano, Mexican if possible
2 cups Shucked oysters or other seafood (shrimp, crab, scallops), en escabeche (to taste)
Strips of canned jalapenos, en escabeche (to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan.
2. Add the onion and garlic cloves and toss over high heat for about 2 minutes without browning.
3. Add the blanched carrots, cauliflower flowerets, marjoram (fresh or dried), oregano, peppercorns, salt, and vinegar to the pan. Cook over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
4. Add the oysters, lime slices, and jalapeno strips (if using) to the pan. Cook for about 2 minutes until the oysters are plump and just cooked.
5. Allow the sauce to cool, then refrigerate overnight.
6. Serve at room temperature. Jalapenos may be omitted and passed separately at the table.
